provenance.notes: Processed 2 quotes from 2 verse files. Source: HistoricalChristianFaith/Commentaries-Database. License: PUBLIC DOMAIN.

data.author: Eustathius of Antioch
data.quote: If, therefore, the Word began to exist at the time he passed through the mother’s womb and wore the bodily framework, it is clear that he was born of a woman. But if God the Word was from the very beginning with the Father, and we say that all things were made through him, then the one who is and is the cause of all things that are made was not born of a woman but is, by nature, God, self-sufficient, unlimited and incomprehensible. But from a woman was born a human being, who was implanted in the virgin’s womb by the Holy Spirit.
data.source_title: DISCOURSE ON THE TEXT “THE LORD CREATED ME IN THE BEGINNING OF HIS WAYS.”

data.author: Eustathius of Antioch
data.quote: For the human being who died rises up on the third day; but when Mary strives with longing to touch his holy limbs, he objected and says to her, “Do not touch me, for I have not yet ascended to my Father; go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, my God and your God.’ ” God the Word, who comes from heaven and lives in the bosom of the Father, did not utter the phrase “I have not yet ascended to my Father.” The Wisdom that embraces all things that exist did not say it either; this was spoken by the very human being who was formed out of all kinds of limbs, who had been raised from the dead, and who after death had not yet ascended to his Father but reserved for himself the firstfruit of his passage.
data.source_title: DISCOURSE ON THE TEXT “THE LORD CREATED ME IN THE BEGINNING OF HIS WAYS.”
